hero.label: AI-Native Data Platform Engineer
hero.title: Denis IL
hero.subtitle:
I build AI-native analytics systems —
from scalable data platforms
to autonomous AI agents.

about.label: About
about.statement: I engineer data systems where AI drives decisions, not just displays them.
about.description: With a focus on AI-native BI, I design end-to-end analytics infrastructure — from raw data ingestion to semantic layers that LLMs reason over.

aboutPage.title: Denis IL
aboutPage.bio: I'm a data engineer and AI systems architect specialising in AI-native analytics infrastructure. My work sits at the intersection of data engineering, semantic modelling, and LLM systems — building platforms where AI reasons over data, not just visualises it.

items.title: AI-Native Analytics Systems
items.description: End-to-end pipelines from raw ingestion to LLM reasoning layers and autonomous reporting agents.

items.title: Data Warehouse Architecture
items.description: Scalable DWH design with ClickHouse, PostgreSQL, Apache Iceberg — built for both BI and AI workloads.

items.title: Semantic Data Layers
items.description: Structured semantic models that enable LLMs to query business data reliably without hallucinating SQL.

items.title: Analytics Engineering
items.description: dbt-based transformation pipelines, data quality frameworks, and governed metric layers.

items.title: Autonomous AI Workflows
items.description: LangChain and Claude-powered agents that monitor KPIs, detect anomalies, and generate insights autonomously.

approach.label: Approach
approach.heading: How I think about data
approach.text: Most BI tools are built to answer questions humans already know to ask. I build systems that find the questions — and answer them — before anyone thinks to look.

principles.title: AI-first, not AI-added
principles.description: Architecture decisions are made with LLM reasoning in mind from day one, not retrofitted later.

principles.title: Semantic over ad-hoc
principles.description: A well-defined semantic layer beats a thousand ad-hoc SQL queries every time.

principles.title: Autonomy over automation
principles.description: The goal isn't to automate dashboards. It's to remove the human from the feedback loop entirely.

availability.label: Availability
availability.description: Available for AI-native data projects, consulting, and architecture reviews.

philosophy.label: Philosophy
philosophy.heading: Beyond the Dashboard
philosophy.description: Traditional BI gives humans a chart. AI-native analytics closes the loop — from raw data to autonomous decisions.

projects.label: Selected Work
projects.heading: Projects
projects.description: Case studies in AI-native analytics, data platform architecture, and autonomous systems.

items.title: AI Analytics Assistant
items.description: Natural language interface over ClickHouse with RAG-based context and LLM reasoning layer. Users ask questions in plain English; the system queries petabytes of data and returns insights.

items.title: Gaming Club DWH
items.description: Multi-source data warehouse for a gaming network — Airflow ingestion pipelines, dbt transformation models, ClickHouse analytical storage, and a Metabase reporting layer.

items.title: Tournament Intelligence
items.description: Real-time analytics platform for esports tournaments. Kafka-powered event stream, live stat aggregation, AI commentary generation, and per-match performance reports.

items.title: Sports Analytics Lab
items.description: Player performance modelling and predictive analytics using historical match data. DuckDB for fast local exploration, Claude for narrative generation.

items.title: ISS Telemetry Analytics
items.description: Telemetry data pipeline and anomaly detection system for orbital station sensor streams. Apache Iceberg lakehouse with S3 storage and Kafka streaming ingestion.

stack.label: Tech Stack
stack.heading: The Stack
stack.description: End-to-end data infrastructure — from ingestion pipelines to AI inference layers.

labs.label: Analytics Labs
labs.heading: The Lab
labs.description: Experiments in autonomous analytics, semantic data models, and AI-native reporting.

items.title: Semantic SQL Layer
items.description: Teaching LLMs to query DWHs through a structured semantic layer instead of raw SQL.
items.longDescription: LLMs are remarkably good at reasoning over structured data — when given the right abstraction. This experiment builds a semantic layer that maps business concepts to SQL constructs, allowing Claude to generate reliable, governed queries without hallucinating column names or joining the wrong tables.

items.title: Autonomous Report Agent
items.description: Agent that monitors KPI deviations and generates executive summaries without human prompting.
items.longDescription: A LangChain agent that runs on a schedule, queries the DWH for KPI deviations, decides if they're significant, and writes an executive summary with root-cause hypotheses. No human in the loop — the agent decides what to report and when.

items.title: Vector DWH Index
items.description: Embedding business metrics into vector space for similarity search and anomaly clustering.
items.longDescription: What if you could find similar business situations from history without writing a query? This experiment embeds aggregated metrics as vectors in ClickHouse, enabling semantic similarity search across time periods, cohorts, and segments.
